unit.name: Implicit composition, level sets, and voxelization
unit.description: Shows how implicit functions can be composed, blended, discretized as level sets, and finally binarized into voxels.
unit.summary: Before switching to deep learning, the lecture extends implicit geometry by showing why it is compositional: unions, intersections, and differences can be implemented by arithmetic or logical operations on function values, which is why CAD systems can assemble complex manufactured parts from simpler pieces [ts=1264s][ts=1292s][ts=1340s]. Signed distance functions add more semantic meaning because the value also encodes distance to the surface, allowing smooth interpolation and blending rather than only hard Boolean composition [ts=1351s][ts=1396s]. Wu then explains level-set thinking: instead of evaluating a closed-form function on demand, one can prequery a dense 3D grid and store values in a volume, effectively turning an implicit field into a nonparametric grid representation while still recovering boundaries from sign changes [ts=1447s][ts=1516s]. If those stored values are binarized to inside/outside occupancy, the result is a voxel grid—a 3D matrix that is easy to understand and later becomes the first deep-learning-friendly 3D representation [ts=1594s][ts=1619s][ts=1647s].

key_points.text: Implicit functions are easy to compose with unions, intersections, and differences.
Distance functions support smooth blending because their values encode distance to the surface.
Level sets precompute implicit values on a dense grid while preserving boundary information through sign changes.
Voxels are binarized occupancy values on a 3D grid, analogous to pixels in 2D.

kp_catalog.name: Implicit functions for geometry
kp_catalog.description: A representation in which surface points satisfy a condition like f(x,y,z)=0, enabling inside/outside reasoning and later neural field methods.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because implicit functions for geometry formalizes how a representation in which surface points satisfy a condition like f(x,y,z)=0, enabling inside/outside reasoning and later neural field methods, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Level sets and voxels
kp_catalog.description: Grid-based discretizations of implicit fields, with voxels storing binarized occupancy values on a 3D lattice.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because level sets and voxels clarifies grid-based discretizations of implicit fields, with voxels storing binarized occupancy values on a 3D lattice, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
